data.title: Trump Promises New White House Correspondents' Dinner Soon — WSJ
data.description:
By Melissa Korn

President Trump said in a Truth Social post that the White House Correspondents' Association dinner would be rescheduled within 30 days. "I am ready, willing and able," he said at his press conference.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Incident Won't Deter Trump From 'Winning The War in Iran' — WSJ
data.description:
By Yoko Kubota

President Trump said that he doesn't believe the suspect was motivated by the Iran war, though he added that he doesn't know for certain. He suggested certain U.S. achievements could make him a target.

"It doesn't happen to people that don't do anything," he said at a press conference after the shooting. "It's not going to deter me from winning the war in Iran," he said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Democrat Thanks Top Republican for Pulling to Secure Room — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Rep. Jared Moskowitz (D., Fla.) thanked law enforcement and top Republican lawmaker Steve Scalise (R., La.) "who grabbed me into a secure room." Scalise was nearly killed in a shooting at a baseball team practice in Alexandria, Va., in 2017.

The U.S. Capitol Police said the members of Congress who attended the White House Correspondents' Dinner are safe and secure.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump Was Sitting Beside the First Lady, WHCA President at Dinner — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

During the dinner, President Trump sat beside White House Correspondents' Association president Weijia Jiang and first landy Melania Trump.

The head table also included White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt, celebrity mentalist Oz Pearlman and officers from the WHCA board.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump Expresses Confidence in Secret Service — WSJ
data.description:
By Sabrina Rodriguez

President Trump praised the Secret Service for their response to the shooter's presence at the dinner. Asked if he was concerned about the Secret Service's protection ability, Trump said, "No, they acted very quickly." Trump said he was impressed by the Secret Service.

"You don't have to complain for me, I would be complaining. I'd be up here right now saying they didn't do their job," he added. "Oh, believe me, because, you know, it's my life, and I want to live because I want to make this country great."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Suspect Likely 'Lone Wolf,' Was Far From Main Event, Trump Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Yoko Kubota

President Trump said he believes the suspect was likely acting on his own. "My impression is he was a lone wolf," he said.

Trump also said that the suspect was far from the room where the event was taking place, and the ballroom was very secure.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: LendingClub (LC) Q1 Earnings: What To Expect
data.description:
Digital lending platform LendingClub will be announcing earnings results this Monday afternoon. Here’s what to look for.

LendingClub beat analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $266.5 million, up 22.7% year on year. It was a strong quarter for the company, with and full-year EPS guidance beating analysts’ expectations.

Is LendingClub a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.

This quarter, the market is expecting LendingClub’s revenue to grow 14.4% year on year, slowing from the 20.5% increase it recorded in the same quarter last year.

Heading into earnings, analysts covering the company have grown increasingly bearish with revenue estimates seeing in majority downward revisions over the last 30 days. LendingClub has a history of exceeding Wall Street’s expectations.

Looking at LendingClub’s peers in the consumer finance segment, some have already reported their Q1 results, giving us a hint as to what we can expect. FirstCash delivered year-on-year revenue growth of 25.7%, beating analysts’ expectations by 4.8%, and Enova reported revenues up 17.4%, topping estimates by 2.8%. FirstCash traded up 3.3% following the results while Enova was down 1.6%.

Read our full analysis of FirstCash’s results here and Enova’s results here.

There has been positive sentiment among investors in the consumer finance segment, with share prices up 13.1% on average over the last month. LendingClub is up 26% during the same time and is heading into earnings with an average analyst price target of $22.50 (compared to the current share price of $17.46).

data.title: Washington Hilton Was Scene of Attempted Reagan Assassination — WSJ
data.description:
By Tim Hanrahan

The Washington Hilton was the scene of the attempted assassination of President Ronald Reagan in 1981, who was shot and wounded by John Hinckley Jr. outside an entrance to the building after a speaking engagement at the hotel. That same entrance was used today for attendees lining up for the red carpet and others entering the White House Correspondents' Dinner event. The incident Saturday night was deeper into the hotel, inside the security perimeter but outside the large auditorium where President Trump sat at the head table.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Journalists Report on Shooting From Correspondents' Event — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

Reporters in the ballroom were working from the dinner after the shooting incident at the White House correspondents' event, fielding calls, filming video footage and filing updates.

"I saw all of you reporting, and that's what we do," White House Correspondents' Association Weijia Jiang told reporters, explaining the event would be rescheduled. "Thank you for coming together tonight."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump Flanked By Cabinet Members at Press Conference — WSJ
data.description:
By Sabrina Rodriguez

President Trump stood at the lectern in the White House's press briefing room to deliver his comments, flanked by a number of administration officials. As he began his remarks, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin and FBI Director Kash Patel stood beside him.

He later invited Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to speak about the investigation and then took questions from reporters. During his remarks, Trump referred to Vice President JD Vance, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Secretary of State Marco Rubio who were also in the room present.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump Thought Loud Bangs Were a Tray Falling — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani

President Trump said he didn't realize at first that the loud bangs in the hall at the White House Correspondents' Dinner were gun shots and that he should have ducked quicker.

"I thought it was a tray coming down," he said at a press conference at the White House. He said first lady Melania Trump realized it sooner, recalling that she said, "That's a bad noise."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.description:
Professional staffing firm Kforce will be reporting results this Monday after market close. Here’s what you need to know.

Kforce beat analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $332 million, down 3.4% year on year. It was a strong quarter for the company, with an impressive beat of analysts’ EPS guidance for next quarter estimates and revenue guidance for next quarter exceeding analysts’ expectations.

Is Kforce a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.

This quarter, the market is expecting Kforce’s revenue to be flat year on year, improving from the 6.2% decrease it recorded in the same quarter last year.

The majority of analysts covering the company have reconfirmed their estimates over the last 30 days, suggesting they anticipate the business to stay the course heading into earnings. Kforce has missed Wall Street’s revenue estimates multiple times over the last two years.

Looking at Kforce’s peers in the professional services segment, some have already reported their Q1 results, giving us a hint as to what we can expect. Robert Half’s revenues decreased 3.8% year on year, meeting analysts’ expectations, and ManpowerGroup reported revenues up 10.3%, topping estimates by 2.1%. Robert Half traded down 5.8% following the results while ManpowerGroup was up 1.3%.

Read our full analysis of Robert Half’s results here and ManpowerGroup’s results here.

There has been positive sentiment among investors in the professional services segment, with share prices up 12.2% on average over the last month. Kforce is up 13.4% during the same time and is heading into earnings with an average analyst price target of $39 (compared to the current share price of $32.15).

ONE MORE THING: The $21 AI Application Stock Wall Street Forgot. While Wall Street obsesses over who’s building AI, one company is already using it to print money. And nobody’s paying attention.

AI chip stocks trade at ridiculous valuations. This company processes a trillion consumer signals monthly using AI and trades at a third of the price. The gap won’t last. The institutions will figure it out. You need to see this first. Read the FREE Report Before They Notice.

data.title: Lake City Bank (LKFN) Reports Earnings Tomorrow: What To Expect
data.description:
Regional banking company Lakeland Financial (NASDAQGS:LKFN) will be reporting earnings this Monday before market open. Here’s what to look for.

Lake City Bank beat analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $70.91 million, up 9.6% year on year. It was a strong quarter for the company, with a decent beat of analysts’ revenue estimates and a beat of analysts’ EPS estimates.

Is Lake City Bank a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.

This quarter, the market is expecting Lake City Bank’s revenue to grow 8.1% year on year, in line with the 7.7% increase it recorded in the same quarter last year.

Analysts covering the company have generally reconfirmed their estimates over the last 30 days, suggesting they anticipate the business to stay the course heading into earnings. Lake City Bank has a history of exceeding Wall Street’s expectations.

Looking at Lake City Bank’s peers in the regional banks segment, some have already reported their Q1 results, giving us a hint as to what we can expect. OFG Bancorp delivered year-on-year revenue growth of 4.2%, beating analysts’ expectations by 4.8%, and Republic Bancorp reported a revenue decline of 11.3%, falling short of estimates by 4.8%. OFG Bancorp traded up 7.6% following the results while Republic Bancorp was also up 1.4%.

Read our full analysis of OFG Bancorp’s results here and Republic Bancorp’s results here.

There has been positive sentiment among investors in the regional banks segment, with share prices up 8.6% on average over the last month. Lake City Bank is up 5.6% during the same time and is heading into earnings with an average analyst price target of $64 (compared to the current share price of $59.68).

data.title: Trump Says Venue Wasn't Secure — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani

President Trump said dinner's venue-the Washington Hilton hotel about a mile-and-a-half from the White House-isn't secure enough and promoted his plans to build a ballroom at the White House.

"It's not a particularly secure building," Trump told reporters at the White House. "We need the ballroom," he added, noting that the ballroom would have bulletproof glass.

Trump has begun construction of a ballroom at the White House, after demolishing the East Wing.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump: Suspect lives in California — WSJ
data.description:
By Yoko Kubota

President Trump said that the shooting suspect at the White House Correspondents' Dinner lives in California.

Officials are going to his apartment, he said. "He's a sick person, very sick person," Trump said. "We don't want things like this."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Charges Will Be Filed Soon, Says Todd Blanche — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani

Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche said charges against the suspected shooter at the White House Correspondents' Dinner will be filed soon. He said the charges will be based on the shooting and the possession of firearms. "I promise you justice will be served," said Blanche.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Dinner Guests Evacuate, Leaving Coats and Bags Behind — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

As guests for the White House correspondents dinner were made to evacuate from the Hilton, members of staff told guests they were unable to retrieve their possessions from coat-check. Reporters left behind their coats and bags as they filed out of the hotel.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: PRES TRUMP/BRIEFING ROOM: GOING TO HOLD CORRESPONDENTS' DINNER AGAIN VERY SOON, BIGGER AND BETTER THAN EVER
data.description: This news item displays a headline only and has no other text.

data.title: Trump Initially Wanted Dinner to Continue — WSJ
data.description:
Trump initially indicated he wanted the dinner to continue. But in a Truth Social post shortly after 9:30 p.m. Eastern time, about an hour after being whisked offstage, he said in a social-media post that law-enforcement officials requested they leave the premises, He said they would reschedule the event within the next month.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
